registry.description: Blessing is the positive flow of divine favour and goodness toward a person, community, or the world — and the human act of speaking that favour over others and back to God. The Hebrew and Greek vocabulary covers both the bestowing of benefit (God blessing) and the spoken declaration that invokes or honours that benefit (humans blessing). Blessing has a social and physical concreteness in the Old Testament: to be blessed is to have children, land, prosperity, and peace. In the New Testament it is spiritualised without being removed from the concrete: blessed are those who are poor in spirit.

mti.status_note: ba.rakh: to bless (occ=330, 249 verses) — blessing as the active conferral of peace and wellbeing. Already linked to H1293 (be.ra.khah: blessing, already extracted). The verb form. The Aaronic blessing (Num 6:24-26) pronounces peace through blessing. Core vocabulary.
